repositories.brief_summary: Standard library additions with Array.stable_sort_sub, String.includes, and Seq.delay, plus documentation improvements and Windows bug fixes
repositories.new_features_summary: Three new stdlib functions merged: Array.stable_sort_sub, String.includes, and Seq.delay for lazy sequence construction
repositories.new_features:
- **Array.stable_sort_sub** function added to sort subarrays in-place using stable sorting algorithm ([#13343](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13343)) by [François Pottier](https://github.com/fpottier)
- **String.includes** function added to test if a substring occurs anywhere in a string ([#14439](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14439)) by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli)
- **Seq.delay** function added to enable proper lazy evaluation in recursive sequence constructors ([#10177](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10177)) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)
- **Raw identifier syntax** documented in manual allowing `#keyword` to use keywords as regular identifiers ([#14468](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14468)) by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb)
- **Effect keyword** added to manual's keyword list with documentation on `-keywords` flag for modifying available keywords ([#14468](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14468))
- **Windows socketpair race condition** fixed preventing address-in-use errors during concurrent socketpair creation ([#14461](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14461)) by [Jessie Grosen](https://github.com/jmgrosen)
- **Substring search micro-optimization** in stdlib improving string matching performance ([#14463](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14463)) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)
repositories.activity_summary: Four new PRs opened including continuation optimization, stdlib workflow automation, runtime events cleanup, and design guidelines
repositories.activity:
- **Four new PRs opened** this week with [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk) proposing continuation size optimization ([#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14469)), [@redianthus](https://github.com/redianthus) adding workflow for automatic stdlib PR labeling ([#14467](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14467)), [Stephen Sherratt](https://github.com/gridbugs) addressing runtime events cleanup on SIGINT ([#14470](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14470)), and [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) proposing stdlib design guidelines ([#14459](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14459))
- **Tooling improvements** merged with [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) associating UIDs to items without concrete definitions for better IDE support ([#14120](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14120))
- **Testing and quality** addressed through [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) fixing TSan test references after earlier changes ([#14466](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14466)), [Sidharth Kshatriya](https://github.com/sidkshatriya) improving debugger printer tests ([#14464](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14464)), and [Boning](https://github.com/bn-d) correcting typo in local opens documentation ([#14471](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14471))
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussions on raw identifiers, String API functions, and Seq.delay design with multiple reviewers providing feedback
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Raw identifier syntax and effect keyword** documentation generated substantial discussion ([#14468](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14468)) with 10 comments reviewing the syntax specification and manual clarity
- **String.includes function** underwent detailed API design review ([#14439](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14439)) with 8 comments discussing naming conventions and partial application efficiency
- **Seq.delay implementation** received thorough examination ([#10177](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10177)) with 9 comments from reviewers analyzing lazy evaluation semantics and use cases
- **Array.stable_sort_sub** reviewed extensively ([#13343](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13343)) with 9 comments covering algorithm correctness and API design
- **Windows socketpair race condition** discussed with 7 comments ([#14461](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14461)) analyzing TOCTOU race conditions and testing approaches
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on stdlib expansion with string manipulation functions and enhanced documentation for language features
repositories.emerging_trends:

- **Platform-specific bug fixes** addressing Windows-specific issues like the socketpair race condition ([#14461](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14461))
- **Tooling infrastructure** being enhanced for IDE support with better shape indexing for items without concrete definitions ([#14120](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14120))
- **Process automation** proposed with workflow for automatic labeling of stdlib PRs ([#14467](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14467))

repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on symbolic link directory handling affecting go-to-definition and find-all-references.
repositories.notable_discussions: - **Path resolution issues** with symbolic link directories reported by [Andrew Helwer](https://github.com/ahelwer) causing go-to-definition to open files with inconsistent path prefixes and find-all-references to fail across subdirectories ([#1582](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1582)). The issue describes how `~/src` symlinked to `/mnt/data/src` creates two path aliases for each file, with LSP sometimes resolving to the `/mnt` prefix instead of `~/` prefix, breaking cross-subdirectory navigation and reference finding

repositories.brief_summary: Action tracing library release, virtual library installation fix, extensive Melange single-context work, test suite reorganization, and build system refactoring
repositories.new_features_summary: dune-action-trace public library, dune trace commands, virtual library installation fix, and nanosecond timestamp support
repositories.new_features:
- **dune-action-trace public library** - new public library allowing custom actions to emit trace events ([#13348](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13348))
- **Virtual library installation fix** - fixed bug #10635 where virtual libraries with public modules caused installation errors ([#13345](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13345))
- **Nanosecond timestamps in traces** - higher precision timestamps for trace events ([#13332](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13332))
- **%{pkg-self:build-id} variable** - new variable for accessing package build identifiers ([#13312](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13312))
- **dune trace command suite** - new command suite for working with trace files ([#13183](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13183))
- **Action tracing via DUNE_ACTION_TRACE_DIR** - environment variable support for tracing custom actions ([#13302](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13302))
- **Improved git error messages** - better error reporting for git operations ([#13309](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13309))
- **--target-exec flag** - new flag for execution targeting ([#13112](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13112))
- **feature: asynchronous digests part #1** ([#13340](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13340))
- **feature: add a debug event to dune** ([#13338](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13338))
- **feature: digest events** ([#13334](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13334))
- **feature: replace debug flags with traces** ([#13330](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13330))

repositories.trends_summary: Action tracing infrastructure becoming production-ready, Melange compilation architecture maturation, systematic test organization, and build system modernization
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Action tracing becoming production-ready** with the release of the `dune-action-trace` public library, new `dune trace` commands, DUNE_ACTION_TRACE_DIR environment variable support, and nanosecond precision timestamps, enabling comprehensive build performance analysis ([#13366](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13366), [#13357](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13357), [#13348](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13348), [#13330](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13330), [#13183](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13183), [#13302](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13302), [#13300](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13300), [#13267](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13267))
- **Melange single compilation context architecture** progressing with extensive refactoring to make compilation modes first-class throughout the codebase, affecting Lib_info, module paths, object directories, and dependency resolution ([#13368](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13368), [#13356](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13356), [#13355](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13355), [#13354](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13354), [#13352](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13352), [#13350](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13350), [#13347](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13347), [#13291](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13291), [#13274](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13274))
- **Test suite reorganization initiative** systematically moving tests into topic-specific subdirectories (promotion, runtest, etc.) and cleaning up test artifacts for better maintainability ([#13367](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13367), [#13363](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13363), [#13361](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13361), [#13359](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13359), [#13360](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13360), [#13343](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13343), [#12857](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12857), [#13326](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13326))
- **Build system architecture cleanup** with continued refactoring including defunctionalizing action builder, merging configuration modules (dune_config + stdune), and streamlining internal APIs ([#13344](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13344), [#13362](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13362), [#13358](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13358), [#13353](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13353), [#13349](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13349), [#13339](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13339), [#13335](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13335), [#13331](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13331))

repositories.brief_summary: Discussion on labeled tuple projections RFC with focus on polymorphic index projection approach
repositories.activity_summary: Ongoing discussion on labeled tuple projections RFC with detailed technical feedback
repositories.activity: - Continued discussion on **labeled tuple projections** RFC ([#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/57)) originally proposed by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) in September 2025, with [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) providing detailed feedback on polymorphic index projection as an alternative approach that would enable more flexible tuple access patterns similar to polymorphic objects and variants
repositories.discussions_summary: Technical debate on tuple projection polymorphism and type system implications
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) presented detailed analysis favoring **polymorphic index projection** over labeled projection in [#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/57), noting that while labeled projections are useful they cannot be polymorphic, whereas index projection would enable tuple length polymorphism consistent with OCaml's existing structural polymorphism for objects and variants, with discussion of implementation complexity, principality requirements for label ordering, and the tradeoffs between ML-style polymorphism versus type propagation approaches
repositories.contributors_summary: Jacques Garrigue provided technical analysis on tuple projection RFC
repositories.contributors: - [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) contributed detailed technical feedback on the labeled tuple projections RFC ([#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/57)), analyzing the relative merits of polymorphic index projection versus labeled projection, discussing type system implications, and providing guidance on implementation approach

repositories.brief_summary: Shell completion installation, Wasm runtime marshal fix, and JavaScript parser issue opened
repositories.new_features_summary: Shell completion support and Wasm marshal compressed data fix
repositories.new_features:
- **Shell completion** installation for `js_of_ocaml` and `wasm_of_ocaml` commands via cmdliner-generated scripts, bumping cmdliner requirement to 1.3.0 ([#2140](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2140))
- **Enhanced autocompletion** for `--enable`, `--disable`, and `--debug` flags with smart token-based completion for optimization and debug section names ([cef7934](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/cef7934a174922db15ee2e991386af781d8569fb))
- **Wasm runtime fix** for unmarshalling compressed data that incorrectly read data size from the wrong location in compressed marshal headers ([#2141](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2141))
repositories.activity_summary: JavaScript parser modernization PR opened, trunk support issue created, and number type conversion discussion
repositories.activity:
- **JavaScript parser modernization** PR opened by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) to align the parser with ECMAScript spec and fix arrow function parsing in for loop definitions ([#2143](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2143), fixes [#2139](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2139))
- **OCaml trunk support** enhancement issue opened by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) to track compatibility with upcoming OCaml compiler changes ([#2142](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2142), references [ocaml/ocaml#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14469))
- **Number type conversion** discussion closed after [@tari3x](https://github.com/tari3x) sought guidance on updating code affected by new number type changes from [#1650](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1650) ([#2136](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2136))
- **Dune workaround** implemented to address issue [dune#13307](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13307) when installing multiple share_root targets with cmdliner completion scripts ([4e91eec](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/4e91eec4ed7d38b7a94d3a864124891b89c87b07))
repositories.discussions_summary: Cmdliner completion Windows compatibility and Dune share_root install bug

repositories.brief_summary: ARM64 backend improvements, mode system refinements, stdlib additions, and testing infrastructure enhancements
repositories.new_features_summary: New Sys.io_buffer_size API, ARM64 typed DSL, chamelon minimizers, and switch optimization improvements
repositories.new_features:
- Exposed `Sys.io_buffer_size` constant in stdlib providing access to the IO buffer size used by runtime system and Unix library primitives ([#5078](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5078))
- ARM64 backend now uses **typed DSL** with constraints enforced by OCaml's type system, improving type safety for instruction generation ([#5193](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5193))
- Chamelon test minimizer gained new capabilities: transforms single-pattern matches into lets and adds `reduce-pat` minimizer for removing match branches ([#5212](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5212))
- Fixed **switch optimization** for constant lookup tables that previously caused quadratic code size growth under `-Oclassic -inline 20` ([#5200](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5200))
- Mode system improvements: functor currying now properly constrains modes for soundness ([#5230](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5230)), and mode solver bug causing non-commutativity in meets/joins was resolved ([#5233](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5233))
- Separability merged into layouts extension, enabling zero-alloc `Array.get` in upstream-compatible code ([#5231](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5231))
- Effect primitives refactored to eliminate `Must_not_enter_gc` special case handling ([#4901](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4901))
- Fixed `Sys.getenv_opt` to preserve backtraces instead of discarding them ([#5076](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5076))
- Added `Invalid` construct through Flambda → Cmm → Cfg → Linear pipeline, avoiding special case handling ([#5208](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5208))
- Documentation updates: fixed mode syntax examples ([#5235](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5235)) and added disclaimer to unboxed types proposal ([#3492](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3492))
repositories.activity_summary: Ongoing work on native expect tests, SIMD vector arrays, layout polymorphism, and long-running mode documentation
repositories.activity:
- **Native expect test infrastructure** opened by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) with three related PRs: `expect.opt` for native toplevel testing ([#5245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5245)), `[%%expect.ignore_echo]` directive to ignore REPL output ([#5244](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5244)), and spacing consistency between native and bytecode toplevels ([#5243](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5243))
- **SIMD vector development** by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) continues with support for arrays of unboxed vec128 pairs ([#5239](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5239)) and translation of wide vectors to tuples on ARM ([#5240](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5240))
- **Layout polymorphism** work progressing through [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) merging `Pmakemixedblock` into `Pmakeblock` ([#5238](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5238)) and adding slambda constructors ([#5148](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5148))
- **Mode/modality typedtree annotations** under active development by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) with extensive design discussions between contributors about proper type representation ([#5218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5218))
- [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) extracted interference graph to standalone module for register allocator reuse ([#5237](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5237))
- **Runtime improvements** include [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) improving `Gc.stat` and `Gc.quick_stat` accuracy for runtime 5 ([#5226](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5226))
- Long-running PRs seeing continued activity: modality documentation by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) ([#4074](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4074)), stdlib stateless annotations ([#4839](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4839)), `Lambda.layout` usage in `transl_exp` ([#4582](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4582)), and level preservation in `Typeopt.value_kind` ([#4549](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4549))
- Flambda2 work by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) and [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) addressing reaper issues and inlining heuristics ([#5157](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5157), [#5108](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5108))
- [George Shammas](https://github.com/georgyo) working on enabling CI tests in Nix builds ([#5228](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5228))
repositories.discussions_summary: Design discussions on mode/modality representation and ARM64 DSL implementation details
repositories.notable_discussions:
- Extensive design iteration on **mode/modality typedtree representation** between [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) and [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) covering whether to use lists vs records, naming conventions, and correspondence with parsetree structures ([#5218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5218))
- ARM64 typed DSL implementation review with detailed feedback from [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) and [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) on register type handling, logical immediate encoding return types, and assertion helpers ([#5193](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5193))
- Discussion on **base non-value kinds crossing externality** by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) and [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) weighing UX improvements through implications against consistency with modal bounds ([#5162](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5162))
- Debate over usefulness of `[%%expect.ignore_echo]` directive with [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) questioning whether thrown-away information is valuable for verification ([#5244](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5244))
- [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) questioned marking operations using physical equality as stateful/observing, discussing implications for atomics and locks ([#4839](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4839))
repositories.trends_summary: Strong focus on ARM64 backend maturity, type safety improvements, and testing infrastructure expansion
repositories.emerging_trends:

- **Testing infrastructure expansion** with native expect tests ([#5245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5245)), chamelon minimizer improvements ([#5212](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5212)), and CI additions ([#5229](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5229))

- **Compiler backend improvements** including switch optimizations ([#5200](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5200)), effect primitive refactoring ([#4901](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4901)), and register allocator modularization ([#5237](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5237))

repositories.brief_summary: Package updates with Dune 3.21.0+ox, Lwt 6.0.0+ox additions, build performance optimization, and new libraries added
repositories.new_features_summary: Dune 3.21.0+ox, Lwt 6.0.0+ox packages, compiler build concurrency fix, and new libraries including handled_effect
repositories.new_features:
- **Dune 3.21.0+ox packages** added with complete build toolchain including dune, dune-configurator, dune-build-info, dune-action-plugin, dune-rpc, dune-rpc-lwt, dune-site, dune-glob, dune-private-libs, chrome-trace, fs-io, stdune, dyn, ocamlc-loc, ordering, and top-closure ([#29](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/29))
- **Lwt 6.0.0+ox packages** including lwt, lwt_direct, lwt_ppx, and lwt_runtime_events, partially upstreamed to [ocsigen/lwt#1096](https://github.com/ocsigen/lwt/issues/1096) ([#30](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/30))
- Compiler **build concurrency optimization** with Dune package management by unsetting `INSIDE_DUNE` environment variable before building, reducing build time from 32 minutes to 14 minutes ([#25](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/25))
- **js_of_ocaml-toplevel build fix** by removing obsolete `[%%if]` patch that was causing extension errors ([#26](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/26))
- **New libraries added**: handled_effect.v0.18~preview.130.83+317 (renamed from oxcaml_effect), memtrace.v0.18~preview.130.83+317, oxcaml_intrinsics.v0.18~preview.130.83+317, unboxed_datatypes.v0.18~preview.130.83+317, hardcaml_hobby_boards_demos, hardcaml_hobby_boards_kernel, and hardcaml_template_project
- **Package version updates** for over 100 Jane Street packages from v0.18~preview.130.76+222 to v0.18~preview.130.83+317 including core, async, bonsai, ppx_* packages, and related libraries
- **Build tooling updates**: ocamlfind 1.9.8+ox, notty-community 0.2.4+ox with title save/restore patch, and reduced patches for js_of_ocaml 6.0.1+ox, mdx 2.5.0+ox, re 1.14.0+ox, and utop 2.16.0+ox
- **CI configuration update** removing macOS 13 testing from GitHub workflows
repositories.activity_summary: Repository maintenance focused on package version bumps, new library additions, and build optimization improvements
repositories.activity:
- **Package version synchronization** with over 100 Jane Street library packages updated from v0.18~preview.130.76+222 to v0.18~preview.130.83+317 including abstract_algebra, accessor family, async family, bonsai family, core family, ecaml, ppx_* preprocessors, and supporting libraries ([b3499cfa](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/commit/b3499cfa424fb869291011282b0ce3eea1f56cf7), [dfe8f731](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/commit/dfe8f731a39e71be96488f3e18d8829d25a92681), [8e8a148c](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/commit/8e8a148c1f4faf459f6c373c9a0e5d702c8b5ee3), [7ca3809b](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/commit/7ca3809b9903fe69a25201e5eed2b335aa2e252f), [03e85afb](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/commit/03e85afb02ac18b0fb7dfabe30c418850eeea0de))
- **Platform updates** including macOS-specific build fixes for await library and removal of macOS 13 from CI testing targets ([e7600975](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/commit/e7600975f7739be1f9c3a8317c4de5ac58873d3a), [34243c8b](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/commit/34243c8ba4cb3ab11e0728293a178e227aef1293), [a3a1d6dd](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/commit/a3a1d6ddaa7bfe95b53adec26b77b9cea234bf53))
- **Patch maintenance** updating oxcaml-dune patches for Dune 3.20.2+ox packages and reducing patch count for several packages by removing upstream-merged changes ([c66d0c97](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/commit/c66d0c974520485bf987ab4d488118344beda288))
- **Library reorganization** with oxcaml_effect renamed to handled_effect and new supporting libraries added for low-level operations
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on Jane Street ecosystem updates and build performance optimization for OxCaml compiler toolchain
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Systematic Jane Street package updates** moving forward with v0.18 preview releases, with version 130.83+317 representing active development of effect handlers and related features
- **Build toolchain improvements** targeting Dune package management integration with focus on reducing unnecessary build restrictions and improving parallelism
- **Package name consolidation** as seen with oxcaml_effect transitioning to handled_effect, aligning with upstream naming conventions
- **Patch reduction strategy** removing patches as changes are upstreamed to original projects (lwt, js_of_ocaml, mdx, re, utop), reducing maintenance burden

repositories.brief_summary: Hash table fixes, Lwt 6.0.0 upgrade, and new experimental lock-free hash table work
repositories.new_features_summary: Fixed hash table resize bug and upgraded Lwt integration to version 6.0.0
repositories.new_features:
- Fixed `picos_aux.htbl` **size adjustment during resize** to properly handle pending resize operations, preventing race conditions during concurrent resizing ([#384](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/384))
- Upgraded **Lwt integration** to version 6.0.0 with safer notification handling using `option` types instead of sentinel values ([#383](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/383))
- Added new **fill1m benchmark** for hash table performance testing with 250,000 operations per domain ([#385](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/385))
repositories.activity_summary: Experimental lock-free hash table development underway alongside infrastructure improvements
repositories.activity:
- Draft PR opened for **experimental lock-free hash table** with incremental resizing by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic), adding 797 lines across 10 files ([#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/382))
- **Hash table implementation** improvements merged including resize bug fix that addresses concurrent size adjustment races ([#384](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/384)) and new fill1m benchmark for performance validation ([#385](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/385))
- **Dependency upgrade** to Lwt 6.0.0 merged with refactored notification system using safer option types ([#383](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/383))
repositories.trends_summary: Focus on lock-free data structure development and infrastructure modernization
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Lock-free data structures** development continuing with experimental hash table implementation featuring incremental resizing for better concurrency ([#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/382))
- **Type safety improvements** in Lwt integration replacing sentinel integer values with explicit `option` types for notification IDs ([#383](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/383))
- **Benchmarking infrastructure** expansion with new fill1m test specifically targeting bulk insert performance patterns ([#385](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/385))
repositories.contributors_summary: All activity by Vesa Karvonen across infrastructure, bug fixes, and experimental features
repositories.contributors: - [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) authored all changes this week including experimental lock-free hash table implementation ([#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/382)), hash table resize fix ([#384](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/384)), Lwt 6.0.0 upgrade ([#383](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/383)), and new hash table benchmark ([#385](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/385))

repositories.brief_summary: Feature requests for API improvements and ongoing work on MacOS block device support
repositories.activity_summary: One PR remains open for MacOS block device support while two feature requests sparked API discussions
repositories.activity:
- **MacOS block device support** PR by [Aaron Eline](https://github.com/aaronjeline) remains under review with discussion about switching from `poll()` to `select()` for handling POLLNVAL on block devices ([#827](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/827), originally addressing [#757](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/757))
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) opened feature request to add `missing_ok` parameter to `Eio.Path.unlink` similar to `rmtree`, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) indicating the request seems reasonable ([#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828))
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) requested **Fmt convenience functions** in Flow and/or Buf_write for easier formatted output, which [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) pointed out is already available via `Buf_write.printf` ([#829](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/829))
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussions focused on API ergonomics for file operations and formatted output
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **File deletion API enhancement** discussion where [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) proposed adding an optional `missing_ok` parameter to `Eio.Path.unlink` to allow deletion without requiring file existence, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) suggesting adding the case to the existing exception handler ([#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828))
- **Formatted output convenience** discussion where [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) sought easier integration with Fmt for production code output, leading to discovery that `Buf_write.printf` already provides this functionality ([#829](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/829))
- **MacOS platform compatibility** discussion on [#827](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/827) exploring implementation approaches (switching from `poll()` to `select()` globally vs maintaining separate thread) for handling block devices that report POLLNVAL on MacOS
repositories.trends_summary: Community focus on API ergonomics and cross-platform file I/O compatibility
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **API convenience improvements** theme emerging with requests for more ergonomic interfaces around existing functionality ([#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828), [#829](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/829))
- **Platform-specific I/O challenges** continuing to surface, particularly around MacOS compatibility with block devices requiring different polling strategies ([#827](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/827), [#757](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/757))
- **Documentation discovery** pattern where users request features that already exist, suggesting potential for improved API discoverability ([#829](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/829))
repositories.contributors_summary: Three contributors active with feature requests and PR reviews for MacOS compatibility
repositories.contributors:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) opened two feature requests: one for adding `missing_ok` parameter to `Eio.Path.unlink` ([#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828)) and another seeking Fmt convenience functions which led to discovering existing `Buf_write.printf` functionality ([#829](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/829))
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) provided maintainer feedback on both feature requests, indicating the `missing_ok` parameter seems reasonable ([#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828)) and pointing to existing `Buf_write.printf` documentation for formatted output ([#829](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/829))
- [Aaron Eline](https://github.com/aaronjeline) continued work on MacOS block device support PR with discussion about implementation approaches for handling POLLNVAL ([#827](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/827))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Active week with Dune 3.21.0 and Lwt 6.0.0 releases across repositories, 11 new packages in opam-repository, and website content updates
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dune 3.21.0, Lwt 6.0.0, new packages (menhir-lsp, qcaml, proven), and website tutorial corrections
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Dune 3.21.0** released across 17 packages in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29216](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29216)), with release announcement opened for [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3453](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3453))
- **Lwt 6.0.0** concurrent programming library released with 4 packages by [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29184](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29184)), with compatibility constraints addressed by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29219](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29219))
- **menhir-lsp 0.2** language server protocol implementation for Menhir parser generator added by [@dalps](https://github.com/dalps) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29203](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29203))
- **qcaml 0.1.7** quantum computing library added by [Elias](https://github.com/elias-utf8) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29229](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29229))
- **proven 0.9.0** formally verified safety primitives library opened by [Jonathan D.A. Jewell](https://github.com/hyperpolymath) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29228](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29228))
- **passe 0.1.0** password hashing library added by [Syaiful Bahri](https://github.com/syaiful6) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29209](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29209))
- **intel_hex 0.1** parser for Intel HEX binary format added by [Михаил](https://github.com/dx3mod) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29180](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29180))
- **binsec 0.11.0** binary analysis platform released by [Frédéric Recoules](https://github.com/recoules) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29193](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29193))
- **containers 3.17** data structures library released by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29217](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29217))
- **multicore-magic 2.3.2** data structures for multicore OCaml released by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29241](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29241))
- **testo 0.3.4** testing framework released across 4 packages by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29239](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29239))
- **ufind 0.2.0** union-find data structure library added by [珊瑚](https://github.com/sanette) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29240](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29240))
- **Tutorial spelling correction** fixed "feets" to "feet" in [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) by [Jørgen Boganes](https://github.com/jorgenboganes) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3452](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3452))
- **Security team page update** added GitHub ID for Boning Dong to OCaml Security Response Team page by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3450](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3450))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **Package ecosystem growth** with 11 new packages added to [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) including developer tooling (menhir-lsp), formally verified libraries (proven), quantum computing (qcaml), and web frameworks (passe)
- **Major release coordination** with Dune 3.21.0 spanning 17 packages in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29216](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29216)) and release announcement prepared for [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3453](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3453))
- **Website content updates** in [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) including tutorial corrections, security team information, and community blog aggregation from OCaml Weekly News
- **Version compatibility work** following Lwt 6.0.0 release with conflict resolution between lwt and mirage-runtime ([ocaml/opam-repository#29219](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29219))
- **Infrastructure challenges** with documentation build failure for Lwt 6.0.0 traced to opamh compilation issues ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3451](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3451))
group_summaries.activity_summary: Version compatibility updates, platform release backfilling, and 15 new PRs opened including LLVM 20/21 support and maintenance policy changes
group_summaries.activity:

- **Additional package releases** including miou 0.5.1 concurrency library ([ocaml/opam-repository#29210](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29210)), art 0.3.0 adaptive radix tree ([ocaml/opam-repository#29208](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29208)), lambda-term 3.3.3 terminal manipulation library ([ocaml/opam-repository#29221](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29221)), server-reason-react 0.4.1 server-side React rendering ([ocaml/opam-repository#29220](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29220)), and 10 packages from ocurrent 0.7.3 CI/CD framework ([ocaml/opam-repository#29191](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29191))
- **Menhir ecosystem updates** with 5 packages from Menhir 20260112 release ([ocaml/opam-repository#29211](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29211))
- **OCaml compiler improvements** with corrected OCaml 5.5.0 compiler variable generation for relocatable installations ([ocaml/opam-repository#29224](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29224), [ocaml/opam-repository#29085](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29085))
- **Maintenance policy proposal** to set default x-maintenance-intent to "(latest)" ([ocaml/opam-repository#29074](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29074))
- **Community content aggregation** with OCaml Weekly News for January 13, 2026 and five blog posts from Tunbury covering OCaml on Raspberry Pi Pico 2 W, opam 2.5 migration, and ARM64 updates ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3454](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3454), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3449](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3449), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3448](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3448))
