group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Version bound management** emerged as key theme with multiple PRs in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) adjusting upper and lower bounds following Dune 3.21.0 release ([ocaml/opam-repository#29213](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29213), [ocaml/opam-repository#29230](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29230), [ocaml/opam-repository#29236](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29236)) and Menhir 20260112 incompatibility ([ocaml/opam-repository#29225](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29225))
- **OCaml 5.x compiler ecosystem maturation** with relocatable OCaml improvements for 5.5.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29224](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29224)), upper bounds for F* on OCaml 5.4 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29230](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29230)), and ongoing LLVM 20/21 support work ([ocaml/opam-repository#29110](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29110))
- **Multicore and concurrency libraries** with releases of multicore-magic 2.3.2 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29241](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29241)), miou 0.5.1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29210](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29210)), Lwt 6.0.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29184](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29184)), and domainpc 0.1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29223](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29223))
- **Formal verification and type safety** focus with proven 0.9.0 formally verified safety primitives ([ocaml/opam-repository#29228](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29228)), restricted 1.2.0 refinement types ([ocaml/opam-repository#29233](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29233)), and binsec 0.11.0 binary analysis ([ocaml/opam-repository#29193](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29193))
- **Web and frontend tooling** with server-reason-react 0.4.1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29220](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29220)) and styled-ppx 0.61.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29227](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29227)) demonstrating continued React and CSS-in-JS ecosystem development
- **Testing and developer experience** improvements with testo 0.3.4 testing framework updates ([ocaml/opam-repository#29239](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29239)), menhir-lsp 0.2 language server ([ocaml/opam-repository#29203](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29203)), and discussion around CI testing practices ([ocaml/opam-repository#29166](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29166))
- **Infrastructure reliability** focus across both repositories with documentation generation pipeline attention on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3451](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3451)) and package maintenance policy evolution in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29060](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29060))
- **Content quality and completeness** with tutorial corrections ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3452](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3452)), security team information updates ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3450](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3450)), and platform release backlog addressing ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3444](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3444))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Dune action tracing infrastructure released, Melange single-context work advanced, signature help improvements across editor tooling
group_summaries.new_features_summary: dune-action-trace library, shell completions for js_of_ocaml, Wasm marshal fix, virtual library installation fix
group_summaries.new_features:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Released [dune-action-trace public library](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13348) enabling custom actions to emit trace events, new [dune trace command suite](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13183) for trace file analysis, and [DUNE_ACTION_TRACE_DIR environment variable](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13302) support
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Fixed [virtual library installation bug #10635](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13345) that caused errors when virtual libraries had public modules
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Added [nanosecond precision timestamps](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13332) to trace events, [%{pkg-self:build-id} variable](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13312) for package build identifiers, and [--target-exec flag](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13112) for execution targeting
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: Added [shell completion installation](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2140) for js_of_ocaml and wasm_of_ocaml commands via cmdliner 1.3.0, with [enhanced autocompletion](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/cef7934a174922db15ee2e991386af781d8569fb) for optimization and debug flags
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: Fixed [Wasm runtime bug](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2141) in compressed data unmarshalling that incorrectly read data size from wrong header location
group_summaries.activity_summary: Extensive Melange single-context refactoring in Dune, signature help improvements in Merlin and OCaml-LSP, opam list enhancements
group_summaries.activity:
- **Melange compilation architecture** in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune): [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) advanced single-context compilation with 9 merged PRs making compilation modes first-class throughout the codebase ([ocaml/dune#13368](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13368), [ocaml/dune#13356](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13356), [ocaml/dune#13355](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13355), [ocaml/dune#13354](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13354), [ocaml/dune#13352](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13352), [ocaml/dune#13350](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13350), [ocaml/dune#13346](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13346))
- **Test suite reorganization** in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune): [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) systematically moved tests into topic-specific subdirectories ([ocaml/dune#13367](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13367), [ocaml/dune#13363](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13363), [ocaml/dune#13361](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13361), [ocaml/dune#13359](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13359), [ocaml/dune#13360](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13360), [ocaml/dune#13343](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13343))
- **Build system refactoring** in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune): [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) defunctionalized action builder and merged configuration modules ([ocaml/dune#13344](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13344), [ocaml/dune#13362](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13362), [ocaml/dune#13358](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13358), [ocaml/dune#13339](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13339), [ocaml/dune#13335](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13335))
- **Signature help enhancements**: [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) worked on improvements across [ocaml/merlin#2023](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2023) (behavior fix to prevent parameter looping) and [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1580) (applying ocamlformat to function signatures), with code review from [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos)
- **Test coverage expansion** in [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin): [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) added reproduction cases for historical bugs ([ocaml/merlin#2021](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2021), [ocaml/merlin#2020](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2020), [ocaml/merlin#2022](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2022))
- **Package management features** in [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam): [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) opened PR to add [license field to opam list](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6841) with documentation and test improvements
- **JavaScript parser modernization** in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml): [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) opened [PR #2143](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2143) to align parser with ECMAScript spec and fix arrow function parsing, and created [OCaml trunk support tracking issue](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2142)
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Dune-related work**: [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) PR [#13293](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13293) by [Puneeth Chaganti](https://github.com/punchagan) addresses Windows executable handling, while [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2140](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2140) implemented workaround for [Dune issue #13307](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13307) affecting share_root install targets with cmdliner completion scripts
- **Signature help consistency**: [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) implemented related improvements across [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) and [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) to enhance developer experience with function signature display ([ocaml/merlin#2023](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2023), [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1580))
- **Toolchain integration**: [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) provided guidance on cmdliner integration patterns in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2140](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2140), and [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) helped diagnose Dune share_root bug affecting multiple tools
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **0install solver behavior**: [@tobil4sk](https://github.com/tobil4sk) reported that [builtin-0install solver incorrectly resolves multiple mingw architectures](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6843) (both i686 and x86_64) for conf-zlib and conf-libpcre2-8, while builtin-mccs+glpk correctly selects only x86_64 variants in [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)
- **Package tag discoverability**: [@jrfondren](https://github.com/jrfondren) proposed improvements to [opam package tag visibility](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6842) with analysis showing 126 org:mirage tags and 729 singleton tags, suggesting tag cloud visualization; [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) demonstrated existing column syntax and opened companion opam2web issue
- **Configure script robustness**: Community member reported [configure script failures in Rocky Linux 8 minimal containers](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/pull/101) lacking pre-installed findutils, extending [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)'s configure hardening PR to additional Linux distributions in [ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind)
- **Symbolic link path resolution**: [Andrew Helwer](https://github.com/ahelwer) reported [LSP navigation issues with symlinked directories](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1582) where go-to-definition and find-all-references fail when ~/src symlinks to /mnt/data/src, causing inconsistent path prefix resolution
- **JavaScript parser limitations**: [Spotandjake](https://github.com/spotandjake) discovered [js_of_ocaml parser fails on arrow functions in for loop variable definitions](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2139), a valid ES6 pattern encountered when linking libbinaryen
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Action tracing maturation** in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune): Infrastructure becoming production-ready with public library release, command-line tooling, environment variable support, and nanosecond precision timestamps enabling comprehensive build performance analysis ([ocaml/dune#13366](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13366), [ocaml/dune#13357](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13357), [ocaml/dune#13348](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13348), [ocaml/dune#13330](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13330), [ocaml/dune#13183](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13183))
- **Melange compilation architecture evolution**: Extensive refactoring in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) making compilation modes first-class throughout codebase, affecting Lib_info, module paths, object directories, and dependency resolution ([ocaml/dune#13368](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13368), [ocaml/dune#13356](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13356), [ocaml/dune#13355](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13355), [ocaml/dune#13354](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13354), [ocaml/dune#13352](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13352), [ocaml/dune#13350](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13350))
- **Developer experience improvements**: Shell completion support added to [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2140](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2140)), signature help enhanced across [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) and [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp), and opam list gaining license field display ([ocaml/opam#6841](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6841))
- **WebAssembly runtime stabilization**: Continued bug fixes in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) for data unmarshalling ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2141](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2141)) alongside ongoing work on stack switching effects ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1832](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1832)) and WASI runtime ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831))
- **Test organization initiatives**: Systematic reorganization in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) moving tests into topic-specific subdirectories ([ocaml/dune#13367](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13367), [ocaml/dune#13363](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13363), [ocaml/dune#13361](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13361)) and expansion of historical bug test coverage in [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) ([ocaml/merlin#2021](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2021), [ocaml/merlin#2020](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2020), [ocaml/merlin#2022](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2022))

group_summaries.brief_summary: ARM64 backend maturation, mode system refinements, package ecosystem updates, and tooling improvements
group_summaries.new_features_summary: ARM64 typed DSL, native expect tests, Dune 3.21.0+ox/Lwt 6.0.0+ox packages, and stdlib additions
group_summaries.new_features:
- ARM64 backend now uses typed DSL with constraints enforced by OCaml's type system, improving type safety for instruction generation in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5193](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5193) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)
- Native expect test infrastructure with `expect.opt` tool for native toplevel testing in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5245), `[%%expect.ignore_echo]` directive in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5244](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5244), and spacing consistency improvements in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5243](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5243) by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4)
- Mode system improvements: functor currying now properly constrains modes for soundness in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5230](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5230), mode solver commutativity bug fixed in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5233](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5233), and separability merged into layouts extension enabling zero-alloc `Array.get` in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5231](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5231) by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js)
- Exposed `Sys.io_buffer_size` constant in stdlib providing access to IO buffer size used by runtime system and Unix library primitives in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5078](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5078)
- Chamelon test minimizer gained new capabilities: transforms single-pattern matches into lets and adds `reduce-pat` minimizer for removing match branches in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5212](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5212) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)
- Fixed switch optimization for constant lookup tables that previously caused quadratic code size growth under `-Oclassic -inline 20` in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5200](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5200) by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls)
- Dune 3.21.0+ox packages added with complete build toolchain in [oxcaml/opam-repository#29](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/29) by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)
- Lwt 6.0.0+ox packages including lwt, lwt_direct, lwt_ppx, and lwt_runtime_events in [oxcaml/opam-repository#30](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/30), partially upstreamed to [ocsigen/lwt#1096](https://github.com/ocsigen/lwt/issues/1096)
- Compiler build concurrency optimization achieved 2x speedup (32 to 14 minutes) by unsetting `INSIDE_DUNE` environment variable in [oxcaml/opam-repository#25](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/25) by [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247)
- Effect primitives refactored to eliminate `Must_not_enter_gc` special case handling in [oxcaml/oxcaml#4901](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4901)
- Fixed `Sys.getenv_opt` to preserve backtraces instead of discarding them in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5076](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5076)
- Added `Invalid` construct through Flambda → Cmm → Cfg → Linear pipeline, avoiding special case handling in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5208](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5208) by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic)
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **Core compiler focus**: Significant progress on ARM64 backend with typed DSL foundation in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5193](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5193), mode system refinements across multiple PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5230](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5230), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5233](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5233), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5231](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5231)), and layout polymorphism groundwork in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5238](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5238) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5148](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5148)
- **Testing infrastructure expansion**: Native expect tests introduced via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5245), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5244](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5244), and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5243](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5243), with chamelon minimizer enhancements in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5212](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5212)
- **Package ecosystem updates**: Over 100 Jane Street packages updated to v0.18~preview.130.83+317 in oxcaml/opam-repository, Dune 3.21.0+ox packages added in [oxcaml/opam-repository#29](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/29), and Lwt 6.0.0+ox packages in [oxcaml/opam-repository#30](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/30)
- **Build performance improvements**: Compiler build time reduced by 2x through concurrency optimization in [oxcaml/opam-repository#25](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/25)
- **Editor integration**: Merlin signature help refinements for labelled arguments in [oxcaml/merlin#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205) and function name triggering in [oxcaml/merlin#206](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/206)
- **Active development**: 15+ merged PRs in oxcaml/oxcaml, 3+ merged PRs in oxcaml/opam-repository, multiple ongoing initiatives including SIMD vectors, Flambda2 improvements, and mode documentation
group_summaries.activity_summary: SIMD vector development, layout polymorphism work, Flambda2 improvements, and package synchronization
group_summaries.activity:
- SIMD vector development by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) continues with support for arrays of unboxed vec128 pairs in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5239](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5239) and translation of wide vectors to tuples on ARM in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5240](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5240)
- Layout polymorphism progressing through [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) merging `Pmakemixedblock` into `Pmakeblock` in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5238](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5238) and adding slambda constructors in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5148](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5148)
- Mode/modality typedtree annotations under active development by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) with extensive design discussions in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5218)
- Runtime improvements include [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) improving `Gc.stat` and `Gc.quick_stat` accuracy for runtime 5 in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5226](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5226)
- [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) extracted interference graph to standalone module for register allocator reuse in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5237](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5237)
- Flambda2 work by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) and [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) addressing reaper issues in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5157](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5157), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5134](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5134), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5140](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5140), and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5125](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5125), plus inlining heuristics in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5108](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5108) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5093](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5093)
- Long-running PRs seeing continued activity: modality documentation by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) in [oxcaml/oxcaml#4074](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4074), stdlib stateless annotations in [oxcaml/oxcaml#4839](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4839), and `Lambda.layout` usage in `transl_exp` in [oxcaml/oxcaml#4582](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4582)
- Package version synchronization with over 100 Jane Street library packages updated from v0.18~preview.130.76+222 to v0.18~preview.130.83+317 in oxcaml/opam-repository including abstract_algebra, accessor family, async family, bonsai family, core family, ecaml, ppx_* preprocessors, and supporting libraries
- New libraries added to oxcaml/opam-repository: handled_effect.v0.18~preview.130.83+317 (renamed from oxcaml_effect), memtrace, oxcaml_intrinsics, unboxed_datatypes, and hardcaml-related packages
- js_of_ocaml-toplevel build fix by removing obsolete `[%%if]` patch in [oxcaml/opam-repository#26](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/26) by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w)
- Merlin signature help improvements by [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) from Tarides: preventing signature help from appearing on function names in [oxcaml/merlin#206](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/206) and adding test coverage for labelled arguments in [oxcaml/merlin#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205)
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Package management coordination**: Dune 3.21.0+ox packages added to [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) in [#29](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/29) support compiler build optimization work in [#25](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/25) that improved build times from 32 to 14 minutes
- **Upstream collaboration**: Lwt 6.0.0+ox packages in [oxcaml/opam-repository#30](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/30) are partially upstreamed to [ocsigen/lwt#1096](https://github.com/ocsigen/lwt/issues/1096), demonstrating effort to contribute changes back to upstream projects
- **Patch reduction strategy**: Changes being upstreamed to original projects (lwt, js_of_ocaml, mdx, re, utop) reduce maintenance burden in oxcaml/opam-repository as seen in [oxcaml/opam-repository#26](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/26)
- **Merlin integration**: Mode/modality typedtree annotations work in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5218) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) explicitly targets [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) editor integration improvements
- **Library naming alignment**: oxcaml_effect renamed to handled_effect in oxcaml/opam-repository aligns with upstream naming conventions and improves consistency across the ecosystem
- **Jane Street ecosystem synchronization**: Over 100 packages updated in lockstep to v0.18~preview.130.83+317 across oxcaml/opam-repository ensures compatibility and represents coordinated release management
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- Extensive design iteration on mode/modality typedtree representation between [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) and [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) covering whether to use lists vs records, naming conventions, and correspondence with parsetree structures in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5218)
- ARM64 typed DSL implementation review with detailed feedback from [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) and [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) on register type handling, logical immediate encoding return types, and assertion helpers in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5193](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5193)
- Discussion on base non-value kinds crossing externality by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) and [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) weighing UX improvements through implications against consistency with modal bounds in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5162](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5162)
- Debate over usefulness of `[%%expect.ignore_echo]` directive with [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) questioning whether thrown-away information is valuable for verification in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5244](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5244)
- [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) questioned marking operations using physical equality as stateful/observing, discussing implications for atomics and locks in [oxcaml/oxcaml#4839](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4839)
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **ARM64 backend maturation**: Typed DSL foundation in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5193](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5193), SIMD vector array support in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5239](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5239) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5240](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5240), and comprehensive logical immediate testing demonstrate sustained focus on ARM64 performance and correctness
- **Type system refinements**: Coordinated improvements across modes (functor currying in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5230](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5230), solver fixes in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5233](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5233)), jkinds (separability merge in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5231](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5231), externality crossing in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5162](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5162)), and layout polymorphism foundation work in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5238](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5238) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5148](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5148)
- **Testing infrastructure expansion**: Native expect tests in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5245), chamelon minimizer improvements in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5212](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5212), and CI additions demonstrate investment in test tooling quality
- **Build performance optimization**: Compiler build time improvement from 32 to 14 minutes in [oxcaml/opam-repository#25](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/25) and backend optimizations like switch improvements in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5200](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5200) show focus on developer experience
- **Flambda2 bug fixes**: Multiple reaper-related fixes in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5134](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5134), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5140](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5140), and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5125](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5125) plus inlining decision improvements in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5093](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5093) indicate ongoing stabilization efforts
- **Upstream contribution pattern**: Patches being upstreamed to lwt, js_of_ocaml, and other projects reduces long-term maintenance burden and benefits the broader OCaml ecosystem
- **Jane Street v0.18 preview development**: Version 130.83+317 package updates across ecosystem represent active development of effect handlers and related features with coordinated release management

group_summaries.brief_summary: Standard library expanded with three new functions, documentation improved, Windows bugs fixed, and RFC discussion on tuple projections
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Three stdlib functions merged: Array.stable_sort_sub, String.includes, and Seq.delay for lazy sequence construction
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Array.stable_sort_sub** function added to sort subarrays in-place using stable sorting algorithm ([ocaml/ocaml#13343](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13343)) by [François Pottier](https://github.com/fpottier)
- **String.includes** function added to test if a substring occurs anywhere in a string ([ocaml/ocaml#14439](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14439)) by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli)
- **Seq.delay** function added to enable proper lazy evaluation in recursive sequence constructors ([ocaml/ocaml#10177](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10177)) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)
- **Raw identifier syntax** documented in manual allowing `#keyword` to use keywords as regular identifiers ([ocaml/ocaml#14468](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14468)) by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb)
- **Effect keyword** added to manual's keyword list with documentation on `-keywords` flag for modifying available keywords ([ocaml/ocaml#14468](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14468))
- **Windows socketpair race condition** fixed preventing address-in-use errors during concurrent socketpair creation ([ocaml/ocaml#14461](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14461)) by [Jessie Grosen](https://github.com/jmgrosen)
- **Substring search micro-optimization** in stdlib improving string matching performance ([ocaml/ocaml#14463](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14463)) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **10 PRs merged** in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) with standard library additions, documentation improvements, Windows platform fixes, and tooling enhancements
- **4 new PRs opened** in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) covering continuation optimization ([ocaml/ocaml#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14469)), workflow automation ([ocaml/ocaml#14467](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14467)), runtime events cleanup ([ocaml/ocaml#14470](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14470)), and stdlib design guidelines ([ocaml/ocaml#14459](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14459))
- **Active RFC discussion** in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) on labeled tuple projections ([ocaml/RFCs#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/57)) with [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) providing detailed technical analysis of polymorphic index projection approach
group_summaries.activity_summary: Four new PRs opened including continuation optimization, stdlib workflow automation, runtime events cleanup, and design guidelines
group_summaries.activity:
- **Continuation optimization** proposed by [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk) to improve continuation size handling ([ocaml/ocaml#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14469))
- **Workflow automation** for stdlib PR labeling proposed by [@redianthus](https://github.com/redianthus) to streamline contribution process ([ocaml/ocaml#14467](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14467))
- **Runtime events cleanup** on SIGINT addressed by [Stephen Sherratt](https://github.com/gridbugs) ([ocaml/ocaml#14470](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14470))
- **Stdlib design guidelines** proposed by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) to formalize API design principles ([ocaml/ocaml#14459](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14459))
- **Tooling improvements** merged with [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) associating UIDs to items without concrete definitions for better IDE support ([ocaml/ocaml#14120](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14120))
- **Testing quality** improved through [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) fixing TSan test references ([ocaml/ocaml#14466](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14466)) and [Sidharth Kshatriya](https://github.com/sidkshatriya) enhancing debugger printer tests ([ocaml/ocaml#14464](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14464))
- **Documentation corrections** merged by [Boning](https://github.com/bn-d) fixing typo in local opens documentation ([ocaml/ocaml#14471](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14471))
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Polymorphic tuple projections** debated in [ocaml/RFCs#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/57) with [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) analyzing polymorphic index projection versus labeled projection, discussing type system implications, principality requirements for label ordering, and tradeoffs between ML-style polymorphism versus type propagation approaches
- **Raw identifier syntax and effect keyword** documentation generated substantial discussion in [ocaml/ocaml#14468](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14468) with 10 comments reviewing the syntax specification and manual clarity
- **String.includes function** underwent detailed API design review in [ocaml/ocaml#14439](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14439) with 8 comments discussing naming conventions and partial application efficiency
- **Seq.delay implementation** received thorough examination in [ocaml/ocaml#10177](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10177) with 9 comments from reviewers analyzing lazy evaluation semantics and use cases
- **Array.stable_sort_sub** reviewed extensively in [ocaml/ocaml#13343](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13343) with 9 comments covering algorithm correctness and API design
- **Windows socketpair race condition** discussed with 7 comments in [ocaml/ocaml#14461](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14461) analyzing TOCTOU race conditions and testing approaches
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Standard library string functions** receiving significant attention with multiple contributions from [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) including String.includes merged in [ocaml/ocaml#14439](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14439) this week and related functions like split, replace, and find in adjacent PRs
- **Documentation quality improvements** focusing on language features previously under-documented, particularly the effect keyword and raw identifier syntax in [ocaml/ocaml#14468](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14468)
- **Platform-specific bug fixes** addressing Windows-specific issues like the socketpair race condition in [ocaml/ocaml#14461](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14461)
- **Tooling infrastructure** being enhanced for IDE support with better shape indexing for items without concrete definitions in [ocaml/ocaml#14120](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14120)
- **Process automation** proposed with workflow for automatic labeling of stdlib PRs in [ocaml/ocaml#14467](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14467)
- **Type system extensions** under discussion with RFC on tuple projections exploring polymorphic access patterns in [ocaml/RFCs#57](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/pull/57)

group_summaries.brief_summary: Light week with Picos hash table improvements and Eio API discussions for ergonomics and MacOS support
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Hash table resize bug fix and Lwt 6.0.0 upgrade in Picos
group_summaries.new_features:
- Fixed `picos_aux.htbl` **size adjustment during resize** to properly handle pending resize operations, preventing race conditions during concurrent resizing ([ocaml-multicore/picos#384](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/384))
- Upgraded **Lwt integration** in [ocaml-multicore/picos](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos) to version 6.0.0 with safer notification handling using `option` types instead of sentinel values ([ocaml-multicore/picos#383](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/383))
- Added new **fill1m benchmark** for hash table performance testing with 250,000 operations per domain in [ocaml-multicore/picos](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos) ([ocaml-multicore/picos#385](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/385))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Light activity week with only 2 of 9 repositories showing changes
- [ocaml-multicore/picos](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos) led activity with 3 merged PRs (hash table fixes, Lwt upgrade, benchmark) and 1 draft PR for experimental lock-free hash table by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic)
- [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) saw 1 ongoing PR for MacOS block device support ([ocaml-multicore/eio#827](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/827)) and 2 feature requests focused on API ergonomics ([ocaml-multicore/eio#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828), [ocaml-multicore/eio#829](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/829))
- No activity in [ocaml-multicore/domainslib](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib), [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring), [ocaml-multicore/meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio), [ocaml-multicore/saturn](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/saturn), [ocaml-multicore/eio-trace](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio-trace), [ocaml-multicore/kcas](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/kcas), or [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-iomux](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-iomux)
- Four contributors active: [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) driving Picos development, [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) raising API enhancement requests, [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) providing maintainer feedback, and [Aaron Eline](https://github.com/aaronjeline) continuing MacOS compatibility work
group_summaries.activity_summary: Picos experimental lock-free hash table development underway; Eio MacOS block device PR under review
group_summaries.activity:
- Draft PR opened in [ocaml-multicore/picos](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos) for **experimental lock-free hash table** with incremental resizing by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic), adding 797 lines across 10 files ([ocaml-multicore/picos#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/382))
- **MacOS block device support** PR in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) by [Aaron Eline](https://github.com/aaronjeline) remains under review with discussion about switching from `poll()` to `select()` for handling POLLNVAL on block devices ([ocaml-multicore/eio#827](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/827), originally addressing [ocaml-multicore/eio#757](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/757))
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) opened feature request in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) to add `missing_ok` parameter to `Eio.Path.unlink` similar to `rmtree`, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) indicating the request seems reasonable ([ocaml-multicore/eio#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828))
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) requested **Fmt convenience functions** in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) Flow and/or Buf_write for easier formatted output, which [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) pointed out is already available via `Buf_write.printf` ([ocaml-multicore/eio#829](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/829))
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **File deletion API enhancement** discussion in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) where [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) proposed adding an optional `missing_ok` parameter to `Eio.Path.unlink` to allow deletion without requiring file existence, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) suggesting adding the case to the existing exception handler ([ocaml-multicore/eio#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828))
- **Formatted output convenience** discussion in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) where [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) sought easier integration with Fmt for production code output, leading to discovery that `Buf_write.printf` already provides this functionality ([ocaml-multicore/eio#829](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/829))
- **MacOS platform compatibility** discussion on [ocaml-multicore/eio#827](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/827) exploring implementation approaches (switching from `poll()` to `select()` globally vs maintaining separate thread) for handling block devices that report POLLNVAL on MacOS
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **API convenience improvements** theme emerging in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) with requests for more ergonomic interfaces around existing functionality ([ocaml-multicore/eio#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828), [ocaml-multicore/eio#829](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/829))
- **Lock-free data structures** development continuing in [ocaml-multicore/picos](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos) with experimental hash table implementation featuring incremental resizing for better concurrency ([ocaml-multicore/picos#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/382))
- **Type safety improvements** in [ocaml-multicore/picos](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos) Lwt integration replacing sentinel integer values with explicit `option` types for notification IDs ([ocaml-multicore/picos#383](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/383))
- **Platform-specific I/O challenges** continuing to surface in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio), particularly around MacOS compatibility with block devices requiring different polling strategies ([ocaml-multicore/eio#827](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/pull/827), [ocaml-multicore/eio#757](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/757))
- **Documentation discovery** pattern in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) where users request features that already exist, suggesting potential for improved API discoverability ([ocaml-multicore/eio#829](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/829))
